rules.title: Sample accepted for sandbox planning
rules.summary: The host accepted a local sample path and created a planning artifact.

rules.title: Hyper-V runbook generated
rules.summary: The host generated a reproducible Hyper-V execution plan for review.

rules.title: R0 collector device unavailable
rules.summary: R0Collector could not open the configured driver device; this records driver health rather than malicious sample behavior.

rules.title: R0 collector start failed
rules.summary: The guest agent could not start the optional R0Collector sidecar; this records collection health rather than malicious sample behavior.

rules.title: R0 collector mock driver event
rules.summary: R0Collector emitted a synthetic driver event for JSONL and Guest Agent plumbing tests.

rules.title: Legacy R0 collector protocol placeholder
rules.summary: A legacy R0Collector build opened the driver device without issuing the read-events IOCTL; current builds should emit driverHealth, driverPoll, and driverReadEvents rows instead.

rules.title: R0 driver health queried
rules.summary: R0Collector successfully queried the KSword sandbox driver health or queue status through DeviceIoControl.

rules.title: R0 driver startup heartbeat observed
rules.summary: The kernel driver emitted a startup heartbeat through the R0 event drain path; current builds use a reserved self-test event and later builds may emit driver.load.

rules.title: Command and scripting interpreter
rules.summary: A process command line references a common Windows scripting interpreter.

rules.title: New process observed after launch
rules.summary: The guest agent observed a non-baseline process after sample execution started. Sandbox plumbing helpers are excluded so the finding stays focused on sample-visible process activity.

rules.title: Registry modification observed
rules.summary: A driver or agent event reported a registry write.

rules.title: Run key persistence
rules.summary: A registry creation or update targeted a Windows Run or RunOnce autostart key.

rules.title: Dropped or modified file
rules.summary: The guest agent observed a file creation or modification during execution.

rules.title: File deletion observed
rules.summary: The guest agent or driver event stream reported a file deletion during execution.

rules.title: Executable or script file written
rules.summary: A file creation or modification event wrote an executable, driver, library, or script-like path.

rules.title: Image load metadata observed
rules.summary: An R0 image-load callback row included image or module path metadata for report correlation with process and driver activity.

rules.title: Outbound TCP activity observed
rules.summary: The guest agent observed a new TCP connection after sample execution.

rules.title: Sample still running at timeout
rules.summary: The sample process was still running when the configured analysis window ended. This is common for GUI applications such as Notepad and is reported as execution-state metadata, not malicious behavior by itself.

rules.title: Known packer or UPX-like PE traits
rules.summary: Static analysis found packer tags such as UPX section names, known packer section markers, or packer strings.

rules.title: High entropy or abnormal PE sections
rules.summary: Static analysis found high-entropy or abnormal PE section traits.

rules.title: Embedded URL string observed
rules.summary: Static analysis extracted URL-like strings from the sample. Manifest, schema, and vendor/fwlink URLs are triage metadata, not observed transfer behavior.

rules.title: PE import table present
rules.summary: Static analysis parsed at least one PE import descriptor; imported module/API evidence may appear in interesting strings.

rules.title: Suspicious Windows API import or string
rules.summary: Static analysis found imports or API-like strings commonly used for native Windows execution, memory, persistence, network, or anti-analysis behavior.

rules.title: Process-injection-capable API imports observed
rules.summary: Static analysis found imports or API-like strings associated with cross-process memory, mapping, thread, APC, or context primitives. This is import-only triage, not observed process injection.

rules.title: Network or download API imports
rules.summary: Static analysis found WinINet, WinHTTP, URLMon, or Winsock import evidence that can support network behavior. This is triage-only unless runtime network traffic is observed.

rules.title: Persistence-capable API imports
rules.summary: Static analysis found registry, service-control, or shell execution API imports that can support persistence changes.

rules.title: Anti-analysis API imports or strings
rules.summary: Static analysis found debugger or timing API imports/strings commonly used in sandbox or debugger checks.

rules.title: TLS directory or callback pointer present
rules.summary: Static analysis found a PE TLS directory or callback pointer, which can execute code before the main entry point and is useful to triage for packing or evasion.

rules.title: PE export table present
rules.summary: Static analysis parsed a PE export directory; exported names may appear in interesting strings for DLL triage.

rules.title: COM registration export entry point
rules.summary: Static analysis found DllRegisterServer, DllUnregisterServer, or DllInstall exports that may be invoked through regsvr32-style execution.

rules.title: Service-style export entry point
rules.summary: Static analysis found service-oriented export names such as ServiceMain, useful for DLL/service triage.

rules.title: Windows service persistence registry path
rules.summary: A registry event targeted a Windows service configuration path, which can indicate service-based persistence or privilege escalation setup.

rules.title: Scheduled task persistence command
rules.summary: A process command line referenced scheduled-task creation or registration primitives.

rules.title: Startup folder persistence path
rules.summary: A file creation or modification event targeted a Windows Startup folder path.

rules.title: Temp/AppData or executable file drop
rules.summary: A file write targeted Temp/AppData or used an executable, library, driver, or script extension; the current rule schema treats path fragments as OR conditions.

rules.title: Script file execution command
rules.summary: A process command line referenced a script-like file extension.

rules.title: HTTP or HTTPS activity observed
rules.summary: A normalized HTTP request event was observed during execution.

rules.title: DNS query observed
rules.summary: A normalized DNS query event was observed during execution.

rules.title: Remote thread or cross-process injection event
rules.summary: A normalized process/thread event indicated remote thread creation or cross-process injection-style activity.

rules.title: Debugger or sandbox check observed
rules.summary: A normalized anti-analysis event reported a debugger, sandbox, or timing check.

rules.title: PE resource directory present
rules.summary: Static analysis parsed a PE resource directory; resource type and payload hints may appear in static evidence.

rules.title: Resource payload candidate
rules.summary: Static analysis found RCDATA, HTML, named, unknown, large, or embedded resource data that may carry a staged payload.

rules.title: Embedded PE in resources
rules.summary: Static analysis found MZ/PE-like bytes inside a PE resource data entry.

rules.title: High-entropy resource data
rules.summary: Static analysis found high-entropy resource data, which can indicate compressed or encrypted embedded content.

rules.title: Writable executable or abnormal section
rules.summary: Static analysis found a writable executable section or an abnormal section layout.

rules.title: Dynamic code loading or memory protection APIs
rules.summary: Static analysis found imports or API-like strings used for dynamic code loading, memory mapping, or memory protection changes.

rules.title: Script or process execution API imports
rules.summary: Static analysis found imports commonly used to launch command interpreters, scripts, or child processes.

rules.title: File creation or dropper API imports
rules.summary: Static analysis found imports commonly used to create, copy, move, delete, or write files.

rules.title: Resource extraction API imports
rules.summary: Static analysis found imports commonly used to find, load, lock, or size PE resources before extracting embedded content.

rules.title: Registry persistence API imports
rules.summary: Static analysis found registry APIs that can support autostart or configuration persistence.

rules.title: Service-control persistence API imports
rules.summary: Static analysis found service-control APIs that can create or alter Windows services.

rules.title: Embedded IP-like string observed
rules.summary: Static analysis extracted IPv4-like strings from the sample. Version/resource-shaped values are triage metadata unless corroborated by runtime network telemetry.

rules.title: Embedded Windows path string
rules.summary: Static analysis extracted Windows filesystem path strings that may help triage dropped files or staging locations.

rules.title: Embedded registry path string
rules.summary: Static analysis extracted registry path strings, including service or Run-key paths when present.

rules.title: Embedded persistence path or command string
rules.summary: Static analysis extracted Run-key, Startup-folder, service, or similar persistence-oriented strings.

rules.title: Embedded script or interpreter command string
rules.summary: Static analysis extracted strings referencing scripting interpreters or common command execution utilities.

rules.title: Encoded PowerShell or script command string
rules.summary: Static analysis extracted encoded-command or base64 execution markers in command strings.

rules.title: Living-off-the-land binary string
rules.summary: Static analysis extracted strings referencing signed Windows utilities often used for proxy execution or download helpers.

rules.title: Anti-sandbox or debugger string
rules.summary: Static analysis extracted debugger, sandbox, VM, or analysis-tool strings.

rules.title: R0 collector lifecycle event
rules.summary: R0Collector emitted startup, heartbeat, device-open, or stopped lifecycle telemetry for driver-side collection visibility.

rules.title: R0 collector IOCTL or protocol failure
rules.summary: R0Collector reported an IOCTL, argument, output, or driver protocol error. This is collection-health evidence rather than direct sample behavior.

rules.title: R0 driver process callback event
rules.summary: A kernel process callback row was imported from driver telemetry and should be inspected with the raw event payload.

rules.title: R0 driver file write or rename signal
rules.summary: Driver telemetry reported file write/set-information/rename/delete style operations outside known sandbox output paths and registry hive backing files. Treat this as R0 filesystem telemetry; confirm process attribution before escalating.

rules.title: R0 driver registry mutation signal
rules.summary: Driver telemetry reported registry create, set, delete, or rename operations from kernel callback evidence.

rules.title: R0 driver network signal
rules.summary: Driver telemetry reported a kernel network event, preserving WFP-style evidence for network triage.

rules.title: Windows service creation command
rules.summary: A process command line referenced Windows service creation or service-control primitives.

rules.title: Scheduled Tasks folder file write
rules.summary: A file write targeted the Windows Scheduled Tasks folder, which can indicate task-based persistence outside command-line creation telemetry.

rules.title: IFEO debugger persistence path
rules.summary: A registry event targeted Image File Execution Options debugger configuration, a common event-triggered persistence and hijack location.

rules.title: Winlogon persistence registry path
rules.summary: A registry event targeted Winlogon Shell, Userinit, Notify, or helper DLL values used for logon persistence.

rules.title: Script file dropped or modified
rules.summary: A file creation or modification event wrote a script-like file extension that may be launched by command interpreters or LOLBins.

rules.title: System directory executable write
rules.summary: A file write touched Windows system directories or executable-like extensions; inspect evidence to distinguish benign installers from payload placement.

rules.title: UDP network activity observed
rules.summary: A normalized UDP network event was collected during sample execution.

rules.title: TLS or HTTPS network activity observed
rules.summary: A normalized network event or driver payload referenced TLS/HTTPS or remote port 443.

rules.title: Network event with IP literal
rules.summary: A network event included a remote address field, preserving IP literal evidence for triage.

rules.title: VM, sandbox, debugger, or analysis-tool artifact query
rules.summary: A path-bearing registry, file, or process query referenced VM, sandbox, debugger, or analysis-tool artifacts.

rules.title: Sleep or delay API observed
rules.summary: Telemetry reported sleep, delay, or wait API usage that can be used to evade short sandbox windows.

rules.title: Typed driver registry Run-key persistence
rules.summary: A driver.registry typed payload reported a key path under Run, RunOnce, RunOnceEx, or policy Run autostart locations.

rules.title: Typed driver registry service persistence
rules.summary: A driver.registry typed payload reported a key path below a Windows service configuration hive.

rules.title: Service ImagePath or ServiceDll registry change
rules.summary: A service registry path changed executable, DLL, or failure-command values commonly used to install or hijack Windows services.

rules.title: Scheduled TaskCache registry persistence
rules.summary: A registry event targeted Task Scheduler cache keys used to register or hide scheduled-task persistence.

rules.title: Typed driver registry Scheduled TaskCache persistence
rules.summary: A driver.registry typed payload reported Task Scheduler cache key activity.

rules.title: PowerShell encoded command execution
rules.summary: A process command line used PowerShell encoded-command switches or short aliases frequently used to conceal script payloads.

rules.title: PowerShell evasion or download abuse
rules.summary: A PowerShell command line used bypass, hidden-window, expression-evaluation, or web-download primitives commonly seen in script abuse.

rules.title: Mshta script proxy execution
rules.summary: A process command line invoked mshta with URL, HTA, JScript, or VBScript content for living-off-the-land script execution.

rules.title: Regsvr32 scriptlet proxy execution
rules.summary: A process command line used regsvr32 scriptlet-loading patterns such as scrobj.dll or remote /i: URLs.

rules.title: Script or LOLBin network download command
rules.summary: A command line referenced built-in download helpers or script web-request primitives often used to stage payloads.

rules.title: Windows Script Host execution
rules.summary: A process command line invoked Windows Script Host or launched script extensions handled by WSH.

rules.title: Typed driver outbound network connection
rules.summary: A driver.network typed payload reported outbound or connect-style network activity from the kernel event stream.

rules.title: Typed driver DNS network traffic
rules.summary: A driver.network typed payload referenced DNS protocol or port 53 traffic.

rules.title: Typed driver web protocol or web-port traffic
rules.summary: A driver.network typed payload referenced HTTP/HTTPS protocol labels or common web ports beyond normalized user-mode network rows.

rules.title: System fingerprinting command for anti-analysis
rules.summary: A command line queried OS, BIOS, computer-system, or hardware inventory data commonly used before sandbox or VM checks.

rules.title: Process or driver enumeration for anti-analysis
rules.summary: A command line enumerated running processes, loaded modules, or drivers, which samples often use to look for analysis tools.

rules.title: Hardware or VM registry fingerprint query
rules.summary: A registry query or driver registry event touched hardware, BIOS, ACPI, PCI, or hypervisor-identifying registry paths.

rules.title: Command-line sleep or delay for anti-analysis
rules.summary: A command line invoked sleep, timeout, ping-delay, or wait primitives that can stall short sandbox windows.

rules.title: Downloaded file mark-of-the-web metadata
rules.summary: A file write referenced Zone.Identifier or mark-of-the-web metadata that can indicate content downloaded from an untrusted zone.

rules.title: Browser or download cache file write
rules.summary: A file was created or modified in a common browser download, cache, or user download location used for staged payloads.

rules.title: Execution from download or staging path
rules.summary: A process launched from a user download, temporary, public, or ProgramData staging path commonly used after payload transfer.

rules.title: Downloaded archive or script staged on disk
rules.summary: A newly written file used an archive, disk image, script, or shortcut extension often seen in download-and-execute chains.

rules.title: Downloaded artifact execution chain observed
rules.summary: A normalized higher-level event reported that a downloaded or staged artifact was executed during the same analysis window.

rules.title: Cross-process memory injection primitive
rules.summary: An event referenced cross-process allocation or memory-write APIs frequently used for process injection.

rules.title: Remote thread or APC injection primitive
rules.summary: An event referenced remote-thread, APC, or Windows hook primitives frequently used to execute code in another process.

rules.title: Process hollowing or suspended-process replacement signal
rules.summary: An event referenced suspended process creation, image unmapping, context manipulation, or resume patterns associated with process hollowing.

rules.title: Executable writable memory protection change
rules.summary: A memory event referenced RWX or executable memory protection changes that can precede injected code execution.

rules.title: LoadLibrary-style DLL injection signal
rules.summary: A remote thread, API, or image-load event referenced LoadLibrary/LdrLoadDll style DLL loading often used for DLL injection.

rules.title: WMI event subscription persistence path
rules.summary: A registry or driver registry event referenced WMI permanent event subscription artifacts such as filters, consumers, or bindings.

rules.title: WMI event subscription creation command
rules.summary: A process command line referenced WMI permanent event subscription creation primitives.

rules.title: COM class hijack persistence path
rules.summary: A registry event targeted COM class registration locations that can redirect execution through InprocServer32, LocalServer32, TreatAs, or ScriptletURL values.

rules.title: AppInit DLLs persistence path
rules.summary: A registry event targeted Windows AppInit DLL loading values used for event-triggered DLL persistence.

rules.title: LSA security provider persistence path
rules.summary: A registry event targeted LSA authentication, security, or notification package values that can load credential-facing providers at boot or logon.

rules.title: PsExec or remote service lateral movement command
rules.summary: A process command line referenced PsExec-like tools or Service Control Manager syntax that can create or start services on remote hosts.

rules.title: WinRM or PowerShell remoting command
rules.summary: A process command line referenced PowerShell remoting or WinRM execution primitives that can run commands on remote systems.

rules.title: Admin share or remote copy command
rules.summary: A process command line referenced administrative shares or copy tools commonly used to stage payloads on remote Windows hosts.

rules.title: WMI remote execution command
rules.summary: A process command line referenced WMI or CIM remote execution and discovery switches for another host.

rules.title: Command-line sandbox or analysis artifact lookup
rules.summary: A process command line referenced VM, sandbox, debugger, or analysis-tool process names commonly queried before changing behavior.

rules.title: User activity or interaction check
rules.summary: A process command line referenced recent-document, user-assist, session, mouse, keyboard, or idle-time checks commonly used to detect automated sandboxes.

rules.title: Analysis or security tool termination command
rules.summary: A process command line attempted to terminate common analysis, monitoring, debugger, or packet-capture tools.

rules.title: LSASS memory dump command
rules.summary: A process command line referenced LSASS dump tooling, MiniDump paths, or common dump utilities used for credential theft triage.

rules.title: LSASS process access observed
rules.summary: Process, API, or driver telemetry reported LSASS as the target process or image for access that should be reviewed for credential dumping.

rules.title: Credential store access observed
rules.summary: File, registry, or driver telemetry touched Windows hives, domain database, browser stores, vaults, or password manager paths containing credential material.

rules.title: Credential dumping tool command
rules.summary: A process command line referenced credential dumping tool names or command tokens such as sekurlsa, lsadump, DCSync, Kerberoast, or browser/vault dump modes.

rules.title: Explicit C2 beacon event
rules.summary: A normalized collector emitted an explicit command-and-control or beacon event.

rules.title: C2 indicator fields in network telemetry
rules.summary: HTTP, TLS, or driver network telemetry contained beacon, callback, check-in, tasking, URI, or user-agent fields often used to label C2 activity.

rules.title: DNS tunnel or DGA indicator
rules.summary: DNS telemetry contained TXT/NULL/CNAME records, tunnel labels, DGA classification, or known DNS tunneling tool labels.

rules.title: Suspicious HTTP C2 user agent
rules.summary: HTTP telemetry reported script, library, legacy, or command-line user agents commonly seen in automated beacons and downloaders.

rules.title: TLS SNI, JA3, or certificate metadata observed
rules.summary: TLS telemetry included SNI, JA3/JA3S, certificate hash, or TLS version fields for encrypted web-protocol triage.

rules.title: PCAP artifact imported
rules.summary: A packet-capture summary, packet, or capture-artifact row was imported with bounded parser metadata for report visibility.

rules.title: PCAP protocol summary placeholder
rules.summary: A PCAP protocol summary referenced DNS, HTTP, TLS, TCP, or UDP evidence for report visibility before first-class packet parsing is available.

rules.title: LSASS process access observed
rules.summary: A process, driver, or API event referenced opening or reading lsass.exe, a common precursor to credential dumping.

rules.title: LSASS dump command line
rules.summary: A command line referenced common LSASS dumping utilities, MiniDump exports, or credential-dumping command tokens.

rules.title: SAM/SYSTEM hive credential material access
rules.summary: A file or registry event referenced Windows SAM, SECURITY, or SYSTEM hive material used for offline credential extraction.

rules.title: Registry hive save command for credentials
rules.summary: A process command line used reg save or shadow-copy helpers against credential-bearing registry hives.

rules.title: Browser credential store access
rules.summary: A file event touched common Chromium or Firefox credential database files.

rules.title: Windows credential vault or DPAPI material access
rules.summary: A file or registry event referenced Windows Vault, DPAPI Protect directories, credential files, or policy secrets.

rules.title: Remote scheduled task command
rules.summary: A command line referenced scheduled task creation or execution against a remote system.

rules.title: SMB network port observed
rules.summary: A network or driver event referenced SMB port 445 or legacy NetBIOS file-sharing ports.

rules.title: WinRM network port observed
rules.summary: A network, driver, or PCAP event referenced WinRM ports 5985 or 5986.

rules.title: CPU or hypervisor check API observed
rules.summary: An API or anti-analysis event referenced CPU, hypervisor, or native system information checks used for sandbox fingerprinting.

rules.title: MAC, disk, or device fingerprint command
rules.summary: A command line queried NIC, MAC address, disk, volume, or device inventory commonly used to identify virtualized environments.

rules.title: Sleep duration telemetry observed
rules.summary: A sleep, timeout, or anti-analysis event carried duration fields used to triage time-based evasion evidence.

rules.title: Analysis tool window or process check API
rules.summary: An API event referenced window, process, module, or debugger enumeration primitives often used to detect analysis tools.

rules.title: Low-resource sandbox check placeholder
rules.summary: A system-fingerprint or sandbox-check event referenced CPU, memory, disk, uptime, or display resource fields used in sandbox heuristics.

rules.title: Microsoft Defender disable or exclusion command
rules.summary: A command line attempted to disable Defender features, add exclusions, stop services, or remove definitions.

rules.title: Defender policy registry disable setting
rules.summary: A registry event modified Windows Defender policy values associated with disabled protections or exclusions.

rules.title: Security tool stop or kill command
rules.summary: A command line referenced stopping or killing common Windows security, logging, or EDR-related services and processes.

rules.title: Windows firewall or network defense disable command
rules.summary: A command line disabled firewall profiles, disabled rules, or broadly opened network access.
containsCommandLine: netsh advfirewall set allprofiles state off

rules.title: Windows event log clearing command
rules.summary: A command line attempted to clear Windows event logs or PowerShell logs.

rules.title: AMSI or ETW bypass command string
rules.summary: A command line referenced common AMSI or ETW bypass tokens used to reduce script and telemetry visibility.

rules.title: PCAP network flow observed
rules.summary: A normalized PCAP flow row included endpoint, port, or protocol metadata for report correlation.

rules.title: PCAP HTTP request observed
rules.summary: A normalized HTTP or PCAP HTTP event included endpoint, host, URI, method, content-type, user-agent, or payload metadata.

rules.title: PCAP DNS query observed
rules.summary: A normalized DNS or PCAP DNS event included endpoint, query, or response-code metadata.

rules.title: PCAP TLS ClientHello observed
rules.summary: A normalized TLS or PCAP TLS event included endpoint, SNI, JA3, ALPN, or TLS version metadata.

rules.title: DNS query to dynamic or disposable domain pattern
rules.summary: A DNS event referenced dynamic DNS, disposable, or commonly abused top-level domain fragments for triage.

rules.title: HTTP direct-IP or nonstandard-port placeholder
rules.summary: A web protocol event referenced direct-IP hosts or nonstandard web ports often worth surfacing in sandbox reports.

rules.title: Screenshot artifact captured
rules.summary: The guest screenshot probe captured a desktop image artifact with dimensions or artifact path metadata for behavior review.

rules.title: Screenshot capture skipped
rules.summary: The guest screenshot probe was enabled but could not capture the desktop; reason and diagnostic fields are surfaced for collection quality review.

rules.title: Memory dump artifact captured
rules.summary: The opt-in guest memory dump probe captured a minidump artifact for the launched sample process.

rules.title: Memory dump capture skipped
rules.summary: The opt-in memory dump probe was enabled but could not produce a dump; diagnostics explain whether the root PID, platform, or API call failed.

rules.title: Dropped file artifact copied
rules.summary: The guest artifact collector copied a newly-created sample working-directory file into artifacts/dropped-files for post-run analysis.

rules.title: Dropped file artifact copy skipped
rules.summary: A dropped-file artifact copy candidate was skipped because it was missing, outside scope, invalid, under the output tree, or failed to copy.

rules.title: Executable dropped-file artifact copied
rules.summary: The copied dropped-file artifact path or source path indicates an executable, script, library, driver, or archive payload staged during execution.

rules.title: Guest artifact manifest written
rules.summary: The guest artifact writer produced a manifest that can correlate screenshots, dropped files, memory dumps, and other artifacts with normalized events.

rules.title: Process tree node observed
rules.summary: The process-tree probe emitted a node with parent, root, depth, child-count, or lineage metadata, enabling full descendant review beyond single process-start rows.

rules.title: Process tree root unavailable
rules.summary: The process-tree probe could not locate the expected root process in the snapshot, which may happen after rapid exit or collection timing races.

rules.title: Sample process start failed
rules.summary: The guest executor failed to start the submitted sample or helper process; failure metadata is surfaced because launch failures can mask behavior.

rules.title: Process tree child-count metadata observed
rules.summary: A process-tree node carried child-count metadata for triage of unusually broad sample lineage fan-out.

rules.title: Living-off-the-land binary in process tree
rules.summary: A process-tree node path referenced a common Windows LOLBin used for proxy execution, scripting, installation, or download staging.

rules.title: Process tree execution from user-writable path
rules.summary: A process-tree or process observation referenced execution from Temp, AppData, Downloads, ProgramData, or Public staging paths.

rules.title: Service inventory item created or modified
rules.summary: The system-change probe observed a Windows service created or modified after sample launch.

rules.title: Service diff with suspicious command metadata
rules.summary: A created or modified service diff referenced command shells, script interpreters, or user-writable staging paths in its raw metadata.

rules.title: Scheduled task inventory item created or modified
rules.summary: The system-change probe observed a scheduled task created or modified after sample launch.

rules.title: Scheduled task diff with suspicious target
rules.summary: A created or modified scheduled task launches script interpreters, command shells, or payloads from user-writable staging paths.

rules.title: Startup item inventory entry created or modified
rules.summary: The system-change probe observed a startup registry value or startup-folder item created or modified after sample launch.

rules.title: Startup item executable or script value
rules.summary: A created or modified startup item references an executable, script, library, or user-writable path as its autostart payload.

rules.title: Persistence inventory item deleted
rules.summary: The system-change probe observed a service, scheduled task, or startup item deleted during execution; this can represent cleanup, tampering, or installer rollback.

rules.title: System diff event volume truncated
rules.summary: The process-tree/system-change probe reached a per-kind diff cap and emitted a truncation marker so analysts know additional service, task, or startup changes existed.

rules.title: DNS cache entry added
rules.summary: The network probe observed a new DNS cache entry after sample execution, preserving name, record type, TTL, or raw resolver evidence.

rules.title: DNS cache entry with tunnel-capable record
rules.summary: A new DNS cache entry referenced TXT, NULL, CNAME, tunnel, DGA, or DNS-log tokens commonly used in DNS C2 and tunneling triage.

rules.title: DNS cache dynamic or disposable domain
rules.summary: A new DNS cache entry contained dynamic DNS, disposable, or commonly abused domain/TLD fragments.

rules.title: Netstat collection metadata row observed
rules.summary: The network probe emitted a netstat row with local/remote endpoints, protocol, state, and optional owning PID. Generic observed rows are collection metadata; use netstat-added-connection for post-launch deltas.

rules.title: Netstat connection added after launch
rules.summary: A netstat row appeared only after sample execution, providing endpoint, state, protocol, and PID metadata beyond the TCP diff API.

rules.title: Network listener opened
rules.summary: A TCP or UDP listener appeared after launch, which can indicate bind shells, local proxying, peer-to-peer behavior, or test harness services.

rules.title: Network listener on non-standard suspicious port
rules.summary: A newly-opened TCP or UDP listener used a port often associated with reverse shells, proxying, Tor, testing backdoors, or non-standard command channels.

rules.title: TCP connection closed after launch
rules.summary: A TCP connection present in baseline was gone after execution; this is useful context for short-lived network behavior and cleanup windows.

rules.title: HTTP executable or script download
rules.summary: HTTP or PCAP HTTP metadata referenced executable/script extensions, PE magic, archive payloads, or binary content types associated with staged tools.

rules.title: HTTP POST or check-in beacon pattern
rules.summary: HTTP request metadata referenced POST/PUT methods or common beacon/check-in paths used by automated command channels.

rules.title: DNS-over-HTTPS request indicator
rules.summary: HTTP metadata referenced DNS-over-HTTPS endpoints or application/dns-message content that can hide DNS lookups inside web traffic.

rules.title: HTTP proxy or tunnel method observed
rules.summary: HTTP telemetry referenced CONNECT, Upgrade, WebSocket, or proxy-like fields often used for tunneling through web infrastructure.

rules.title: TLS invalid or self-signed certificate indicator
rules.summary: TLS metadata referenced invalid, expired, untrusted, self-signed, or mismatched certificate validation evidence.

rules.title: TLS ECH or ESNI metadata observed
rules.summary: TLS metadata referenced encrypted SNI or Encrypted ClientHello fields for encrypted-channel triage.

rules.title: PCAP executable payload indicator
rules.summary: Packet-derived metadata referenced PE magic, executable filenames, archive payloads, or binary content types suggesting payload transfer.

rules.title: PCAP DNS NXDOMAIN or DGA indicator
rules.summary: Packet-derived DNS telemetry referenced NXDOMAIN responses, DGA classification, or suspicious generated-domain labels.

rules.title: PCAP upload or exfiltration indicator
rules.summary: Flow or HTTP metadata referenced upload direction or exfiltration labels that can support C2-channel exfiltration triage.

rules.title: PCAP flow-count metadata observed
rules.summary: Packet summary or flow metadata included count, unique-destination, packet, or duration fields for future fan-out and beacon thresholding.

rules.title: RDP network port observed
rules.summary: Network, driver, netstat, or PCAP telemetry referenced the Remote Desktop Protocol port 3389 or protocol labels.

rules.title: SSH network port observed
rules.summary: Network, driver, netstat, or PCAP telemetry referenced SSH port 22 or SSH protocol labels, useful for lateral movement and tunneling triage.

rules.title: LDAP or Kerberos network port observed
rules.summary: Network telemetry referenced LDAP, LDAPS, Kerberos, or Global Catalog ports that can support domain discovery, authentication, and lateral movement triage.

rules.title: Tor or proxy port observed
rules.summary: Network telemetry referenced common local or remote proxy/Tor/SOCKS ports or protocol labels used to relay command channels.

rules.title: Admin share file path observed
rules.summary: A file event referenced administrative shares such as ADMIN$, C$, IPC$, or UNC staging paths commonly used for lateral tool transfer.

rules.title: Remote Desktop command observed
rules.summary: A command line referenced mstsc, cmdkey, or RDP connection file usage that can indicate interactive remote desktop activity.

rules.title: Network share discovery command
rules.summary: A command line enumerated network shares, domains, sessions, or remote computers using built-in Windows discovery tools.

rules.title: System information discovery command
rules.summary: A command line collected local OS, host, user, domain, or computer-system details that often precede staging or evasion decisions.

rules.title: Process discovery command
rules.summary: A command line enumerated running processes or services using tasklist, PowerShell, WMI, or service-control utilities.

rules.title: Network configuration discovery command
rules.summary: A command line collected local network configuration, routes, ARP cache, firewall, or active connection details.

rules.title: Screen capture API observed
rules.summary: An API event referenced GDI/User32 screen-capture primitives such as BitBlt, GetDC, PrintWindow, or GetForegroundWindow.

rules.title: Double-extension masquerading file
rules.summary: A file event referenced document/image/archive-looking names that end in executable or script extensions, a common user deception pattern.

rules.title: Hidden or system file attribute command
rules.summary: A command line used attrib to set hidden, system, or read-only file attributes, often used to conceal staged files or persistence artifacts.

rules.title: Legacy RunServices persistence path
rules.summary: A registry event touched legacy RunServices or RunServicesOnce autostart paths still useful for malware triage on older Windows profiles.

rules.title: AppCert DLLs persistence path
rules.summary: A registry event touched Session Manager AppCertDlls, which can load DLLs into processes that call selected Win32 APIs.

rules.title: Application shim persistence artifact
rules.summary: A registry or file event touched AppCompat shim database registration paths used to redirect application behavior or load attacker-controlled shims.

rules.title: Active Setup persistence path
rules.summary: A registry event touched Active Setup Installed Components or StubPath values that can execute commands during user logon initialization.

rules.title: Logon script persistence registry path
rules.summary: A registry event touched Windows logon/startup script policy paths that can execute attacker-controlled scripts during user or machine logon.

rules.title: BITS job persistence or download command
rules.summary: A command line configured BITS jobs, notification commands, or transfers that can persist or stage payloads without an interactive browser.

rules.title: Screensaver persistence registry path
rules.summary: A registry event modified desktop screensaver configuration values that can launch a malicious SCR payload at idle or unlock time.

rules.title: PowerShell profile persistence file
rules.summary: A file event wrote a PowerShell profile script that can run code every time a PowerShell host starts for the affected user or host.

rules.title: Process handle access for injection
rules.summary: A process access or API event requested VM-write, VM-operation, create-thread, or all-access rights commonly needed before process injection.

rules.title: Thread context hijacking injection primitive
rules.summary: An API or thread event referenced suspending a thread and changing its context, a common execution redirection primitive.

rules.title: Portable executable manual-map injection signal
rules.summary: An event referenced manual PE mapping, RunPE, section-image mapping, or process doppelganging style injection evidence.

rules.title: Reflective loader or module stomping signal
rules.summary: An API, memory, or image-load event referenced reflective DLL loading, in-memory module loading, or module stomping evidence.

rules.title: DCOM remote execution command
rules.summary: A command line referenced DCOM automation classes or helper labels commonly used to execute commands on a remote Windows host.

rules.title: RPC or DCOM endpoint mapper port observed
rules.summary: A network, driver, or PCAP flow event referenced RPC/DCOM endpoint mapper ports or protocol labels often used during remote Windows management.

rules.title: PowerShell SSH remoting command
rules.summary: A PowerShell command line used SSH transport remoting switches that can run commands on a remote system over SSH.

rules.title: Remote registry command observed
rules.summary: A process command line used reg.exe against a remote registry path, which can support remote persistence, configuration change, or discovery.

rules.title: Locale, language, or timezone anti-analysis command
rules.summary: A command line queried locale, language, or timezone information often used to gate execution by region or identify sandbox defaults.

rules.title: User input or idle-state anti-analysis API observed
rules.summary: An API event referenced user input, cursor, foreground window, or idle-time checks used to detect unattended sandbox sessions.

rules.title: Accelerated or skipped sleep telemetry observed
rules.summary: Sleep or timeout telemetry carried requested/actual duration fields that can expose long-sleep evasion or sandbox time acceleration.

rules.title: Certutil download or decode command
rules.summary: A command line used certutil URL-cache, verifyctl, decode, or decodehex options to stage or transform payload data.

rules.title: Msiexec remote package execution
rules.summary: A command line used msiexec to install or execute a remote HTTP/HTTPS/UNC package, a common proxy execution and download-execute pattern.

rules.title: Rundll32 remote URL or script execution
rules.summary: A command line used rundll32 with URL handlers, JavaScript, or remote DLL/script references associated with proxy execution.

rules.title: Archive extracted then executed chain observed
rules.summary: A normalized correlation event indicates an archive or ISO was extracted or mounted and a child payload executed from the extracted path.

rules.title: DNS long-label or base64-like query
rules.summary: DNS telemetry carried long-label, high-entropy, or base64-marker fields associated with tunneling, DGAs, or encoded C2 messages.

rules.title: DNS exfiltration volume metadata observed
rules.summary: DNS or PCAP summary telemetry included query-count or byte-total fields for tunnel and exfiltration triage.

rules.title: HTTP authorization, cookie, or token exfiltration field
rules.summary: HTTP metadata included authorization headers, cookies, bearer tokens, passwords, or session fields that can indicate credential or token exfiltration.

rules.title: TLS missing SNI or rare JA3 indicator
rules.summary: TLS telemetry indicated missing/encrypted SNI or a rare JA3/JA3S classification for encrypted-channel C2 triage.

rules.title: QUIC or HTTP/3 network activity
rules.summary: UDP, flow, or TLS metadata referenced QUIC/HTTP3/ALPN h3 traffic that can carry encrypted command-and-control over web ports.

rules.title: PCAP beacon interval placeholder
rules.summary: PCAP or flow telemetry included beacon interval, jitter, or periodicity fields for future C2 timing correlation.

rules.title: Packet capture artifact indexed
rules.summary: A manifest, host artifact, or PCAP import event recorded a packet-capture artifact path, hash, MIME type, or byte size for report linkage.

rules.title: Office add-in persistence registry path
rules.summary: A registry event targeted Microsoft Office add-in, VSTO, or application option values that can load attacker-controlled code when Office starts.

rules.title: Office startup folder persistence file
rules.summary: A file write targeted Office Startup, Word STARTUP, or Excel XLSTART locations that load templates/add-ins when Office applications start.

rules.title: Accessibility feature IFEO debugger persistence
rules.summary: A registry event configured Image File Execution Options debugger values for Windows accessibility utilities commonly hijacked for persistence or privilege escalation.

rules.title: Logon script persistence file path
rules.summary: A file write targeted Group Policy or user logon/logoff script paths that can execute scripts during interactive logon or startup.

rules.title: Mavinject process injection command
rules.summary: A command line used mavinject.exe injection switches to load a DLL into another process.

rules.title: InstallUtil proxy execution command
rules.summary: A command line invoked InstallUtil, which is commonly abused for .NET signed utility proxy execution in malware sandboxes.

rules.title: MSBuild inline-task proxy execution
rules.summary: A command line referenced MSBuild inline tasks or CodeTaskFactory-style project execution used for trusted developer utility proxy execution.

rules.title: Regasm or Regsvcs proxy execution command
rules.summary: A command line invoked Regasm or Regsvcs for signed utility proxy execution against a .NET assembly.

rules.title: LSASS dump file created
rules.summary: A file creation or artifact copy used an LSASS-oriented dump filename, complementing process-access and command-line LSASS dumping evidence.

rules.title: Clipboard collection API observed
rules.summary: An API-call event referenced Windows clipboard access APIs that can collect copied credentials, tokens, wallet addresses, or operator input.

rules.title: Screenshot-like image file created by sample
rules.summary: A file write used screenshot, screen, desktop, or capture-oriented filenames, which can corroborate screen-capture API evidence.

rules.title: Uptime or boot-time anti-analysis check
rules.summary: A command line queried system uptime, boot time, or elapsed run time, often used to identify freshly started sandbox guests.

rules.title: Parent-process anti-analysis check API
rules.summary: An API-call or process-query event referenced parent-process or PEB inspection primitives used to detect non-interactive sandbox launchers.

rules.title: Windows credential staging command
rules.summary: A command line staged credentials with cmdkey for remote Windows access, commonly adjacent to SMB, RDP, or WinRM lateral movement.

rules.title: Domain trust discovery command
rules.summary: A command line queried domain trusts, domain controllers, or domain groups before potential lateral movement.

rules.title: Tor hidden-service domain observed
rules.summary: DNS, HTTP, or PCAP metadata referenced a .onion hidden-service domain, which can indicate anonymized C2 or proxy infrastructure.

rules.title: HTTP long or encoded URI C2 indicator
rules.summary: HTTP metadata was labeled as a long, encoded, base64, or high-entropy URI, which is useful C2 beacon and tasking triage evidence.

rules.title: HTTP/TLS domain-fronting indicator
rules.summary: HTTP, TLS, or PCAP metadata indicated host/SNI mismatch or explicit domain-fronting classification used to hide C2 destinations behind shared infrastructure.

rules.title: TLS C2 indicator fields
rules.summary: TLS metadata was labeled as beacon, C2, suspicious JA3/JA3S, or suspicious SNI by a guest, PCAP, or enrichment sidecar.

rules.title: Office process spawned script or shell child
rules.summary: Process-tree telemetry showed an Office application spawning a shell, scripting interpreter, or LOLBin child, a strong malicious-document execution pattern.

rules.title: Browser process spawned script, shell, or LOLBin child
rules.summary: Process-tree telemetry showed a browser spawning a shell, scripting interpreter, installer, or proxy-execution utility after download or exploit-driven execution.

rules.title: Run key points to user-writable payload
rules.titleZh: Run 启动项指向用户可写载荷
rules.summary: A Run/RunOnce autostart registry value points at Temp, AppData, ProgramData, Public, Downloads, or another user-writable staging path.
rules.summaryZh: Run/RunOnce 自启动注册表值指向 Temp、AppData、ProgramData、Public、Downloads 或其他用户可写暂存路径。

rules.title: Run key launches script interpreter or LOLBin
rules.titleZh: Run 启动项启动脚本解释器或 LOLBin
rules.summary: A Run/RunOnce autostart value launches PowerShell, Windows Script Host, mshta, rundll32, regsvr32, cmd, or another proxy-execution utility.
rules.summaryZh: Run/RunOnce 自启动值启动 PowerShell、Windows Script Host、mshta、rundll32、regsvr32、cmd 或其他代理执行工具。

rules.title: StartupApproved autostart state modified
rules.titleZh: StartupApproved 自启动状态被修改
rules.summary: A registry event touched StartupApproved Run/Run32 state, which can re-enable disabled autostart entries or hide Run-key state changes.
rules.summaryZh: 注册表事件触及 StartupApproved Run/Run32 状态，可能重新启用被禁用的自启动项或隐藏 Run 键状态变化。

rules.title: Service binary path points to user-writable location
rules.titleZh: 服务二进制路径指向用户可写位置
rules.summary: A service configuration path references an executable or DLL under Temp, AppData, ProgramData, Public, or Downloads, indicating service persistence or hijack risk.
rules.summaryZh: 服务配置路径引用 Temp、AppData、ProgramData、Public 或 Downloads 下的 EXE/DLL，提示服务持久化或劫持风险。

rules.title: Service ImagePath has unquoted-space hijack risk
rules.titleZh: 服务 ImagePath 存在未加引号空格劫持风险
rules.summary: A service ImagePath-like value references Program Files style paths; analysts should verify quoting because unquoted service paths enable path interception.
rules.summaryZh: 服务 ImagePath 类值引用 Program Files 风格路径；分析员应确认是否正确加引号，因为未加引号服务路径可能导致路径拦截。

rules.title: Service failure command persistence
rules.titleZh: 服务失败命令持久化
rules.summary: A service registry or inventory event modified failure-command settings, which can execute attacker-controlled commands when a service fails.
rules.summaryZh: 服务注册表或清单事件修改失败命令设置，服务失败时可能执行攻击者控制的命令。

rules.title: Weak service permission hijack metadata
rules.titleZh: 弱服务权限劫持元数据
rules.summary: Service inventory or ACL telemetry indicated that low-privilege users can change service configuration, binary path, or DACL values.
rules.summaryZh: 服务清单或 ACL 遥测显示低权限用户可修改服务配置、二进制路径或 DACL。

rules.title: Scheduled task COM handler persistence
rules.titleZh: 计划任务 COM Handler 持久化
rules.summary: A scheduled-task registry or inventory event referenced a COM handler action or CLSID-backed task execution path.
rules.summaryZh: 计划任务注册表或清单事件引用 COM handler 动作或基于 CLSID 的任务执行路径。

rules.title: Scheduled task XML targets user-writable payload
rules.titleZh: 计划任务 XML 指向用户可写载荷
rules.summary: A scheduled-task file, registry, or inventory event references a task target under Temp, AppData, ProgramData, Public, or Downloads.
rules.summaryZh: 计划任务文件、注册表或清单事件引用 Temp、AppData、ProgramData、Public 或 Downloads 下的任务目标。

rules.title: Kernel driver service install evidence
rules.titleZh: 内核驱动服务安装证据
rules.summary: Service registry or inventory telemetry referenced a kernel-driver service type or .sys ImagePath, which can indicate privileged driver installation.
rules.summaryZh: 服务注册表或清单遥测引用内核驱动服务类型或 .sys ImagePath，可能表示安装了高权限驱动。

rules.title: Executable launched from user-writable staging path
rules.titleZh: 从用户可写暂存路径启动可执行文件
rules.summary: A process start command line launched from Temp, AppData, Downloads, ProgramData, Public, or Recycle Bin style staging locations.
rules.summaryZh: 进程启动命令行来自 Temp、AppData、Downloads、ProgramData、Public 或回收站等暂存位置。

rules.title: Archive or installer spawned user-writable payload
rules.titleZh: 压缩包或安装器衍生用户可写载荷进程
rules.summary: Process-tree telemetry shows an archive, self-extractor, or installer launching a child from a user-writable staging location.
rules.summaryZh: 进程树遥测显示压缩工具、自解压程序或安装器启动了用户可写暂存位置中的子进程。

rules.title: DLL image loaded from user-writable path
rules.titleZh: 从用户可写路径加载 DLL 镜像
rules.summary: Image-load telemetry referenced a DLL or module path under Temp, AppData, ProgramData, Public, or Downloads, supporting DLL injection or hijack triage.
rules.summaryZh: 镜像加载遥测引用了 Temp、AppData、ProgramData、Public 或 Downloads 下的 DLL/模块路径，可辅助 DLL 注入或劫持排查。

rules.title: Process access with debug or all-access rights
rules.titleZh: 使用调试或全访问权限访问进程
rules.summary: Process-access telemetry requested debug privilege, PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS, VM write, or thread creation rights against another process.
rules.summaryZh: 进程访问遥测请求调试权限、PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS、VM 写入或远程线程创建权限。

rules.title: Token impersonation or duplication API observed
rules.titleZh: 观察到令牌模拟或复制 API
rules.summary: API telemetry referenced token duplication, impersonation, or token-based process creation primitives used for privilege escalation and lateral movement.
rules.summaryZh: API 遥测引用令牌复制、模拟或基于令牌创建进程的原语，可用于提权和横向移动。

rules.title: Auto-elevate UAC bypass registry path
rules.titleZh: 自动提权 UAC 绕过注册表路径
rules.summary: A registry event touched ms-settings, mscfile, exefile runas, or shell open command paths used by auto-elevate UAC bypass chains.
rules.summaryZh: 注册表事件触及 ms-settings、mscfile、exefile runas 或 shell open command 等自动提权 UAC 绕过链常用路径。

rules.title: Auto-elevate UAC bypass LOLBin launched
rules.titleZh: 启动自动提权 UAC 绕过 LOLBin
rules.summary: A command line launched auto-elevating Windows binaries commonly paired with registry hijacks for UAC bypass.
rules.summaryZh: 命令行启动常与注册表劫持配合绕过 UAC 的自动提权 Windows 二进制。

rules.title: PowerShell in-memory reflection loader
rules.titleZh: PowerShell 内存反射加载器
rules.summary: A PowerShell command line referenced reflection, shellcode, PE injection, or memory-protection primitives used for in-memory execution.
rules.summaryZh: PowerShell 命令行引用反射、shellcode、PE 注入或内存保护原语，常用于内存执行。

rules.title: PowerShell spawned LOLBin child
rules.titleZh: PowerShell 衍生 LOLBin 子进程
rules.summary: Process-tree telemetry shows PowerShell spawning proxy-execution utilities such as rundll32, regsvr32, mshta, certutil, bitsadmin, or installutil.
rules.summaryZh: 进程树遥测显示 PowerShell 启动 rundll32、regsvr32、mshta、certutil、bitsadmin 或 installutil 等代理执行工具。

rules.title: CMSTP profile proxy execution command
rules.titleZh: CMSTP 配置文件代理执行命令
rules.summary: A command line invoked cmstp.exe with INF/profile installation flags commonly abused for signed binary proxy execution and UAC bypass chains.
rules.summaryZh: 命令行调用 cmstp.exe 及 INF/配置文件安装参数，常被用于签名二进制代理执行和 UAC 绕过链。

rules.title: Control Panel item proxy execution command
rules.titleZh: 控制面板项代理执行命令
rules.summary: A command line invoked control.exe, rundll32 shell32 Control_RunDLL, or a CPL payload path, which can proxy execution through Control Panel components.
rules.summaryZh: 命令行调用 control.exe、rundll32 shell32 Control_RunDLL 或 CPL 载荷路径，可能通过控制面板组件代理执行。

rules.title: Sensitive privilege enabled
rules.titleZh: 敏感权限被启用
rules.summary: Token or API telemetry enabled SeDebugPrivilege, SeBackupPrivilege, SeRestorePrivilege, SeTakeOwnershipPrivilege, or related privileges commonly used for credential theft or escalation.
rules.summaryZh: 令牌或 API 遥测启用了 SeDebugPrivilege、SeBackupPrivilege、SeRestorePrivilege、SeTakeOwnershipPrivilege 等常用于凭据窃取或提权的权限。

rules.title: Periodic network beacon metadata
rules.titleZh: 周期性网络 Beacon 元数据
rules.summary: Network, HTTP, C2, or PCAP metadata carried beacon interval, jitter, or periodic-check-in fields suitable for C2 triage.
rules.summaryZh: 网络、HTTP、C2 或 PCAP 元数据携带 beacon 间隔、抖动或周期性 check-in 字段，可用于 C2 排查。

rules.title: DNS query to reputation-risk domain
rules.titleZh: DNS 查询命中信誉风险域名
rules.summary: DNS telemetry carried reputation labels for newly registered, suspicious, DGA, malware, or C2 domains.
rules.summaryZh: DNS 遥测携带新注册、可疑、DGA、恶意软件或 C2 域名信誉标签。

rules.title: DNS high-entropy subdomain metadata
rules.titleZh: DNS 高熵子域名元数据
rules.summary: DNS telemetry included label entropy, long-label, or query-length fields used to triage DGA or DNS-tunnel behavior.
rules.summaryZh: DNS 遥测包含标签熵、长标签或查询长度字段，可用于排查 DGA 或 DNS 隧道行为。

rules.title: HTTP JSON tasking or gate URI
rules.titleZh: HTTP JSON 任务或 Gate URI
rules.summary: HTTP metadata referenced JSON tasking, gate, panel, check-in, command, or polling URI patterns often seen in C2 protocols.
rules.summaryZh: HTTP 元数据引用 JSON tasking、gate、panel、check-in、command 或 polling URI 模式，常见于 C2 协议。

rules.title: HTTP host header mismatch indicator
rules.titleZh: HTTP Host 头不匹配指示
rules.summary: HTTP metadata indicated Host/header, SNI, absolute URI, or upstream destination mismatch, useful for proxy, fronting, or evasive C2 triage.
rules.summaryZh: HTTP 元数据指示 Host/header、SNI、绝对 URI 或上游目的地不匹配，可用于代理、域前置或规避型 C2 排查。

rules.title: TLS certificate reputation risk
rules.titleZh: TLS 证书信誉风险
rules.summary: TLS metadata labeled a certificate, thumbprint, issuer, or chain as malicious, suspicious, revoked, abused, or newly observed.
rules.summaryZh: TLS 元数据将证书、指纹、签发者或证书链标记为恶意、可疑、吊销、滥用或新近出现。

rules.title: Root certificate store modification command
rules.titleZh: 根证书存储修改命令
rules.summary: A command line attempted to add or import a certificate into LocalMachine or CurrentUser Root certificate stores.
rules.summaryZh: 命令行尝试向 LocalMachine 或 CurrentUser Root 根证书存储添加或导入证书。

rules.title: Root certificate store registry change
rules.titleZh: 根证书存储注册表变更
rules.summary: A registry or file event touched Windows Root/SystemCertificates certificate store paths.
rules.summaryZh: 注册表或文件事件触及 Windows Root/SystemCertificates 证书存储路径。

rules.title: Debug register or breakpoint check API
rules.titleZh: 调试寄存器或断点检查 API
rules.summary: API telemetry referenced thread context, debug registers, hardware breakpoints, or trap flag checks used to detect debuggers.
rules.summaryZh: API 遥测引用线程上下文、调试寄存器、硬件断点或陷阱标志检查，用于检测调试器。

rules.title: Host identity sandbox check
rules.titleZh: 主机身份沙箱检查
rules.summary: Sandbox-check or fingerprint telemetry referenced hostnames, usernames, domains, or sandbox-user defaults used to gate execution.
rules.summaryZh: 沙箱检查或指纹遥测引用主机名、用户名、域名或沙箱默认用户，用于决定是否执行。

rules.title: VirusTotal malicious verdict
rules.titleZh: VirusTotal 恶意判定
rules.summary: VirusTotal enrichment returned a malicious verdict for the submitted SHA-256. This is external reputation evidence, not a local ATT&CK behavior by itself.
rules.summaryZh: VirusTotal 富化对提交的 SHA-256 返回恶意判定。这是外部信誉证据，本身不是本地 ATT&CK 行为。

rules.title: VirusTotal suspicious verdict
rules.titleZh: VirusTotal 可疑判定
rules.summary: VirusTotal enrichment returned a suspicious verdict without malicious detections; use it as external reputation triage evidence.
rules.summaryZh: VirusTotal 富化返回可疑判定但无恶意命中；应作为外部信誉排查证据使用。

rules.title: VirusTotal hash not found
rules.titleZh: VirusTotal 未收录哈希
rules.summary: VirusTotal returned 404/not_found for the SHA-256. This documents enrichment coverage, not malicious or benign behavior.
rules.summaryZh: VirusTotal 对该 SHA-256 返回 404/not_found。这记录富化覆盖情况，不表示恶意或良性行为。

rules.title: VirusTotal lookup rate-limited
rules.titleZh: VirusTotal 查询被限速
rules.summary: VirusTotal returned a rate-limit status. Reports should show this as enrichment quality metadata instead of a lookup failure with ambiguous meaning.
rules.summaryZh: VirusTotal 返回限速状态。报告应将其显示为富化质量元数据，而不是语义不清的查询失败。

rules.title: VirusTotal API key not configured
rules.titleZh: 未配置 VirusTotal API Key
rules.summary: VirusTotal enrichment was skipped because no API key is configured. This quiet diagnostic should not affect maliciousness verdicts.
rules.summaryZh: 由于未配置 API Key，VirusTotal 富化被跳过。此静默诊断不应影响恶意性判定。
